Must Be An Election Year!](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=a439dc8a6ce940f582c77b167f1d672b&_e=Gf-hKWvnitIZN5Yr1LU9Svzf7ym1HPvmHFw0Yonu7MoWMwlxJ4KTS-Xs-6k6TNBzvFmPJ8X1cytGk27VUNoYZwGjtEdjyvMzj5tjCVfvMbji4-T0EgtksqEjLeBgZPsp0FJYRcv4EbZCDc5YyfqyVYh-ieT0nOPmeAUEHM7MeSqg3jaJE0QCHaBsyHuh6ULC-qoOmZZLt153WGHPQleOAb1iKmHHYXU9opKJ_KV-A42BiKrK1N8aCb7nMp-t2WQOMmsNzpLCzbaTNpPfTfgCcfO6VVVeJk2ToNdOT4lMzqo4us5p2TnvjcQ2xU0FA8npRRHEXtsqfJILh3MuMIL6op4qhKlpzZVHv2BHoxo2ui9EA3r0fP33xANx-n1qhdS3)
Now that it’s 2018, an election year, I would like to welcome Senator Tammy Baldwin back to Wisconsin. Instead of doing the right thing and voting for legislation that would have improved the lives of so many whose voices she is supposed to represent in Washington, Senator Baldwin did what she has done so often during her almost 30 years in public office: She played politics with the well-being and wallets of Wisconsin citizens. It was a desperate attempt to score cheap political points — and not so cheap campaign contributions — from her radical, far left supporters in faraway states.

name: Tom Perez
subject: We only get one shot, Derek
body:
We won't win in November unless we all work together -- starting with our first fundraising deadline of 2018
---
Derek --

In two days, we face our first fundraising deadline of 2018. Let me tell you why this deadline is so important:

This is our opportunity to send an unmistakable message to Trump and the GOP that this country does not accept their divisive and disturbing agenda. It's our chance to get our nation back on track -- we only get one shot and we can't afford to come up short.

We need your help, so I am asking you today:

**Will you make a $3 contribution right now before January's deadline? We're in a critical election year and your support will help Democrats win.**

**[ DONATE: $3](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522239/4067b0a/4ba96aed8/7e50f795/4234369396/VEsEAA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/january-deadline-2018?amount=3&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=25+-+DONATE+3&utm_campaign=em_20180129_tp_v3s3&source=em_20180129_tp_v3s3&refcode=em_20180129_tp_v3s3")
[DONATE: $10](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522239/4067b0a/4ba96aed8/7e50f792/4234369396/VEsEAw/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/january-deadline-2018?amount=10&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=26+-+DONATE+10&utm_campaign=em_20180129_tp_v3s3&source=em_20180129_tp_v3s3&refcode=em_20180129_tp_v3s3")
[DONATE: $25](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522239/4067b0a/4ba96aed8/7e50f793/4234369396/VEsEAg/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/january-deadline-2018?amount=25&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=27+-+DONATE+25&utm_campaign=em_20180129_tp_v3s3&source=em_20180129_tp_v3s3&refcode=em_20180129_tp_v3s3")
[DONATE: $50](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522239/4067b0a/4ba96aed8/7e50f790/4234369396/VEsEDQ/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/january-deadline-2018?amount=50&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=28+-+DONATE+50&utm_campaign=em_20180129_tp_v3s3&source=em_20180129_tp_v3s3&refcode=em_20180129_tp_v3s3")
[DONATE: $100](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522239/4067b0a/4ba96aed8/7e50f791/4234369396/VEsEDA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/january-deadline-2018?amount=100&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=29+-+DONATE+100&utm_campaign=em_20180129_tp_v3s3&source=em_20180129_tp_v3s3&refcode=em_20180129_tp_v3s3")
[Or donate another amount.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522239/4067b0a/4ba96aed8/7e50f79e/4234369396/VEsFBQ/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/january-deadline-2018?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=30+-+Or+donate+another+amount&utm_campaign=em_20180129_tp_v3s3&source=em_20180129_tp_v3s3&refcode=em_20180129_tp_v3s3")
** Taking on Trump and the Republican Party isn't just about taking back Congress this year. It's about building a national movement of Democrats who are prepared to come together and fight for our values. It's about electing leaders who will take that fight from the school board to the Oval Office.

If we do that, we will be unstoppable in 2018, 2020, and beyond. But that starts with creating a strong Democratic Party -- and we won't be able to do that without your support.

**[Contribute $3 ahead of our first fundraising deadline of 2018. Now is the time to show you stand with Democrats.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522239/4067b0a/4ba96aed8/7e50f79f/4234369396/VEsFBA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/january-deadline-2018?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=31+-+Contribute++if+consinConsGroup28497++set&utm_campaign=em_20180129_tp_v3s3&source=em_20180129_tp_v3s3&refcode=em_20180129_tp_v3s3")**

There's a wave coming, Derek. Thanks for being a part of it.

Tom

Tom Perez
Chair
Democratic National Committee

name: The White House
subject: The year in speeches
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b79bc5b4f989d160a9d25b2784d3d358ad59b7a47efbdb7ab711c4b98685ef719edb1aaedad3dda455515b2d07044e7521255fe69f0a4a8b

The year in speeches

Tomorrow night, President Donald J. Trump will address Americans in the first State of the Union address of his presidency. He’ll lay out the biggest milestones from his first year in office—tax reform, a budget that rebuilds the U.S. military, and so on—before explaining his priorities for year two.

Before turning to the next chapter, today we revisit a few of the President’s most important addresses from his first year. There are plenty to choose from, but a few quotes stand out:

- “Now we are calling for a great reawakening of nations, for the revival of their spirits, their pride, their people, and their patriotism.”
- “I declare today for the world to hear that the West will never, ever be broken. Our values will prevail, our people will thrive, and our civilization will triumph.”
- “I have determined that it is time to officially recognize Jerusalem as the capital of Israel.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b79bc5b4f989d160d55f6ed46f5988f4aae1c8ffcb83f7770b1af29e97fa266e4c0a26ea51f3f900550c4dbfec9049abbbd1c8d34b8b6bde
See the Facebook album of President Trump’s “Speeches of Year One.”

POLL: Majority of Americans agree with the President on immigration

As the White House released its framework on immigration reform and border security last week, a recent Harvard-Harris Poll confirms that President Trump’s approach resonates with a majority of Americans.

Saagar Enjeti of The Daily Caller delivers the sentence that Congress should be paying close attention to in the weeks ahead:

“The poll, weighted to be broadly representative of the U.S. population, found that 65% of voters overall agreed with Trump’s position that any bill codifying Obama-era protections for illegal immigrants brought to the U.S. as children should be accompanied by funding for a wall, ending chain migration, and ending the diversity visa lottery program.”

Enjeti continues that “the individual elements of each of Trump’s demands remain broadly popular as well.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b79bc5b4f989d160246ac7bceb1fb952a5eb773e58545624dcb31817525e45978e2a51350c2779679b0881c0f3472c1ba7e577aab6cf4490
Read more: “POLL: Majority of Americans Agree with Trump on DACA, Immigration”

More jobs, more opportunity, more growth

A little more than one year after President Trump took office, the U.S. economy continues to soar to new heights. During the past week, for instance, American workers, businesses, and families have all witnessed headlines that show the American economy is back:

- MARKETWATCH: S&P 500 Breaks 54-Year-Old Record For All-Time Highs Notched In January
- CNBC: The Dow’s 31% Gain During Trump’s First Year Is The Best Since FDR
- THE HILL: Poll: Satisfaction With Economy Reaches 17-Year High
- THE WALL STREET JOURNAL: The Tax Law, Just One Month Old, Is Roaring Through U.S. Companies

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b79bc5b4f989d16032375f9918e67f9332e361ddcdad8df2c92a91c82366e91672f259b0be3b4979deddd63af38ec10e557b42e1bcdb041f
See the full list of news showing more jobs, more opportunity, and more growth.

Photo of the Day

Marine One | January 25, 2018 (Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead)

POTUS TODAY

This morning, President Trump and Vice President Mike Pence will participate in the swearing-in ceremony for the Secretary of the Department of Health and Human Services Alex Azar. The President will then have lunch with leaders from the United Nations Security Council.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b79bc5b4f989d1607c43efcecae269ce9991aa78d8825e9804bde33d2536cf9b0509e58d04393095bebeedc33353a7f614f2c9fc93c82967
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b79bc5b4f989d16007b52682b18c001653c6ebdbba5eaae025433fc0b66aa6fb0eb37a07463c0a3d2bc2b0cc4fbf1edb6829eccc2f41b96e
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d47cabb10ada14b9f89ac79fd483dacc523081fddb753b66cdc953d558b8b3da663074a56cc6100bd0357585cc494b39e8b591b824201b1d79
Unsubscribe

name: Jill Vogel
subject: 2018 Legislative Session Update - January
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Vogel for Senate45 North Hill Drive, Suite 100Warrenton, VA 20186
body:
---
| | | | | |
---
| | |
---
| Dear Peter,
The 2018 session of Virginia's General Assembly convened January 10th. The Senate has returned with a new Governor Ralph Northam and new President of our Senate, Lt. Governor Justin Fairfax. I congratulate them and we are working together now on a number of issues critical for this year's session. In the months leading up to session, we were hard at work preparing legislation and in December, focused on moving into new offices adjacent to the Capitol while the old General Assembly Building will be demolished. Amidst the moving chaos, 2,901 bills and resolutions have been introduced and our biggest challenge, drafting Virginia's next biennial budget, is well underway. Crafting a fiscally responsible and balanced budget is a top priority. As a member of the Senate Finance Committee, our focus should be to prepare a disciplined budget which adequately funds core government priorities, including education, transportation, and public safety. I have introduced a number of bills important to our region, including:

* A bill to fight opioid abuse by establishing a prescription drug takeback program at retail pharmacies;
* Legislation to fight gerrymandering and draw fairer and more compact legislative districts.
* A bill to ban politicians' personal use of campaign funds.
* A bill to expand choice in health care by allowing for more MRI and CT machines in our district.
* A constitutional amendment allowing for legislative review of outdated or overly-burdensome administrative rules.
* A bill to expand health insurance coverage for people with Autism.
* A bill establishing a pilot program to help students with special needs in our public schools.
* A bill to fight opioid abuse by tracking wholesale prescription drug shipments, allowing law enforcement to more quickly spot harmful activity associated with "pill mills."
* A bill legalizing industrial hemp for commercial purposes and helping Virginia become a national leader in creating new agricultural jobs.
* A bill legalizing the use of CBD oil for patients with very serious medical conditions.
* A bill reforming discovery rules to help fight abusive lawsuits.
* A measure to fund additional Sheriff's deputies.
* A measure providing resources for a career and technical education center in Winchester.
* Measures to fund additional Court clerk positions.
* An amendment to provide resources for the critical Career Development Program which aides in retention of experienced staff in Constitutional Offices.
* A measure to fully fund the state's share for Commissioners of the Revenue positions.
* A provision to restore funding for Deputy Treasurers.
* A measure to assist people with disabilities in state employment.
* A measure to provide state surplus hardware to schools in order to implement IT training and certification.
* A provision to study alternative ways to address college debt and financial aide.
* A measure to provide resources for the historic Battlefield Preservation Fund.
* A measure to provide funding for the Laurel Center in Winchester and for services for survivors of domestic abuse and sexual violence.
* A measure to fund replacement of water quality monitoring equipment on the Shenandoah River and its tributaries.
* A measure to provide resources for the State Police Area 13 barracks in Winchester.

I take your thoughts and opinions seriously and urge you to contact our office any time that you have questions or concerns. I can be reached during the General Assembly session at 804-698-7527 or by email at [district27@senate.virginia.gov](mailto:district27@senate.virginia.gov). If you have an opportunity to be in Richmond between now and March 9th, please consider visiting the Capitol and joining me in the Senate as my guest, or visit our office in room E612 of the Pocahontas building. Call ahead to let us know and we will make arrangements to welcome you. Everyone in our office appreciates the opportunity to do this important work and we are grateful for your support.

Sincerely,

clean_body: | | | | | | | | | | Dear Peter, The 2018 session of Virginia's General Assembly convened January 10th. The Senate has returned with a new Governor Ralph Northam and new President of our Senate, Lt. Governor Justin Fairfax. I congratulate them and we are working together now on a number of issues critical for this year's session. In the months leading up to session, we were hard at work preparing legislation and in December, focused on moving into new offices adjacent to the Capitol while the old General Assembly Building will be demolished. Amidst the moving chaos, 2,901 bills and resolutions have been introduced and our biggest challenge, drafting Virginia's next biennial budget, is well underway. Crafting a fiscally responsible and balanced budget is a top priority. As a member of the Senate Finance Committee, our focus should be to prepare a disciplined budget which adequately funds core government priorities, including education, transportation, and public safety. I have introduced a number of bills important to our region, including: A bill to fight opioid abuse by establishing a prescription drug takeback program at retail pharmacies; Legislation to fight gerrymandering and draw fairer and more compact legislative districts. A bill to ban politicians' personal use of campaign funds. A bill to expand choice in health care by allowing for more MRI and CT machines in our district. A constitutional amendment allowing for legislative review of outdated or overly-burdensome administrative rules. A bill to expand health insurance coverage for people with Autism. A bill establishing a pilot program to help students with special needs in our public schools. A bill to fight opioid abuse by tracking wholesale prescription drug shipments, allowing law enforcement to more quickly spot harmful activity associated with "pill mills." A bill legalizing industrial hemp for commercial purposes and helping Virginia become a national leader in creating new agricultural jobs. A bill legalizing the use of CBD oil for patients with very serious medical conditions. A bill reforming discovery rules to help fight abusive lawsuits. A measure to fund additional Sheriff's deputies. A measure providing resources for a career and technical education center in Winchester. Measures to fund additional Court clerk positions. An amendment to provide resources for the critical Career Development Program which aides in retention of experienced staff in Constitutional Offices. A measure to fully fund the state's share for Commissioners of the Revenue positions. A provision to restore funding for Deputy Treasurers. A measure to assist people with disabilities in state employment. A measure to provide state surplus hardware to schools in order to implement IT training and certification. A provision to study alternative ways to address college debt and financial aide. A measure to provide resources for the historic Battlefield Preservation Fund. A measure to provide funding for the Laurel Center in Winchester and for services for survivors of domestic abuse and sexual violence. A measure to fund replacement of water quality monitoring equipment on the Shenandoah River and its tributaries. A measure to provide resources for the State Police Area 13 barracks in Winchester. I take your thoughts and opinions seriously and urge you to contact our office any time that you have questions or concerns. I can be reached during the General Assembly session at 804-698-7527 or by email at mailto:district27@senate.virginia.gov. If you have an opportunity to be in Richmond between now and March 9th, please consider visiting the Capitol and joining me in the Senate as my guest, or visit our office in room E612 of the Pocahontas building. Call ahead to let us know and we will make arrangements to welcome you. Everyone in our office appreciates the opportunity to do this important work and we are grateful for your support. Sincerely, | | Senator Jill Vogel | | | Paid for and Authorized by Vogel for Senate45 North Hill Drive, Suite 100Warrenton, VA 20186 | Follow Jill on Facebook and Twitter for Campaign Updates == == | | Vogel for Virginia | 45 North Hill Drive, Suite 100, Warrenton, VA 20186 | | Sent by mailto:info@senatorjillvogel.com in collaboration with |

name: Tim Kaine
subject: Good news and bad news
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
Derek:

Tomorrow night, Donald Trump will address the nation as he delivers his first State of the Union, and people all across the country will be listening closely. _The thing is, what President Trump says may not really matter at all._

We've learned by now that he'll say whatever he thinks he needs to say in any given moment to push his own agenda (or he'll just say whatever happens to float into his head -- honestly it can be hard to tell the difference at times). And days -- if not minutes -- later, he may contradict himself! But what matters isn't what he says, it's what he does. And we've seen clear as day that he's prepared to enact the worst of his campaign promises.

The good news is, Congress has the power to pull the emergency brake on many of Trump's wackiest and most dangerous plans as they roll down the pike. The bad news: We can only do that if we have the votes. There will be 34 Senate seats up this November, and Democrats have the opportunity to take back the majority and give Americans confidence that we have a real check on this president -- but not if we don't protect the seats we already hold.

**I'm up for re-election, and I'm not taking anything for granted in this race -- so I sure could use your support as I work on building up a smart, tough campaign.**

**Wednesday is our first end-of-month deadline of 2018, and I've set a goal of raising $30,000 by then. Will you pitch in $25 -- or whatever you can afford, because every single dollar counts -- before midnight on January 31st?**

[**Click here to make a quick donation online.**](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=mFWvWzIAGSzcivI%2BYEWx0IFlStGS1lZa)

I'm grateful for your support -- thank you very much.

Tim Kaine

name: Russ Feingold, LegitAction
subject: Two landmark victories already
body:
[ Legit ]LegitAction

Friend, we’re achieving historic victories less than a month
into 2018.

A few weeks ago, our hard work paid off when the sham Trump-Pence
election commission shut down for good. Last week, we helped Florida
voters put an amendment on their 2018 ballot that could restore voting
rights to over 1.5 million citizens.

We can continue to achieve victories like these throughout the rest of
2018, but only if grassroots supporters help fund our work at full
strength. That’s why I’m setting a goal to raise $10,000 by the end of
January to support our work this year.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/go/4846?t=1002&akid=580%2E356062%2EMA1c1W ]Make a contribution of
$3 to help us reach our important
$10,000 grassroots goal before January ends on Wednesday.

The sham Trump-Pence election commission was laying the groundwork for
nationalized voter suppression. But LegitAction supporters rose up and
held the commission publicly accountable until it shut down for good.

Florida has the largest population of disenfranchised former felons in
the country. But thousands of LegitAction supporters helped gather
enough signatures to put their voting rights on the November ballot.

Our victories this month will strengthen the legitimacy of future
elections, including the crucial ones in November. But there's still
so much left to do in the fight for equal voting rights, fair
districts, and legitimate elections. We need to raise more resources
to have maximum impact.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/go/4846?t=1003&akid=580%2E356062%2EMA1c1W ]Help us reach our important $10,000 grassroots goal before the
month ends. Make a contribution of
$3 today.

Together, we already set the tone for a pivotal year in our fight for
democratic legitimacy. I can’t wait to see what we accomplish next.

Thank you for standing up for democratic legitimacy today,

Russ Feingold
LegitAction

subject: Don't let the Trump machine win
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail
body:
| |
---
| |
---
| _Fox News_ ran a story earlier this month called _"Republicans will keep control of Congress this year."_ Now, we're tempted to borrow a phrase from our illustrious president and call this "fake news." But they made one good point. _"Even if the Democrats begin catching up, the Trump machine will turn on the low-dollar digital spigot and turn the GOP fundraising advantage into election victories."_ And if the "Trump machine" decides to turn its laser-eyes on us, we need to be ready. [_**Our very first deadline of the election year is only 2 days away, and we're still about $1,800 short of our $15,000 goal.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113fba/622b7b5b/1901e1a3/307bb755/638301767/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dwse180126?refcode=dwse180126_m&recurring=true&amount=5") Debbie has four opponents. She's already a target for attacks, lies, and smears. Trump himself called her names. Trump and his people aren't huge fans of Debbie and it is understandable. She is a leader who speaks out against their radical agenda. She never shies away from a fight when Florida families stand to lose out. Needless to say, Republicans aren't too fond of that. Debbie doesn't mind. She's going to stand up for what's right and fight for us no matter who attacks her for it. **We need to support her.** [_**Long story short -- we can't miss this goal. If you can, rush in $5 or more.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113fba/622b7b5b/1901e1a3/307bb755/638301767/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dwse180126?refcode=dwse180126_m&recurring=true&amount=5") Thanks! body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c541c/24c69fd9/12a1b3a9b/18956af6/652668474/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFEM50N_digital_email_20180129_EOM598FilingDeadlines_inact&source=WQEFEM50N_digital_email_20180129_EOM598FilingDeadlines_inact")
---

Peter,

Blink, and you'll miss them.

Filing deadlines -- the date by which candidates have to officially declare they're running in order to get their name on the ballot -- are already upon us.

That's why it's critical we have the resources to support the hundreds of pro-choice Democratic women putting their names on the ballot (many of them for the first time) right now. They literally cannot wait.

**[Donate $3 to help reach our $150,000 End-of-Month goal so we can help women across the country launch campaigns.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c541c/24c69fd9/12a1b3a9b/18956af7/652668474/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180128_email_EOMJanuary?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+to+help+reach+our+150000+EndofM&utm_campaign=WQEFEM50N_digital_email_20180129_EOM598FilingDeadlines_inact&source=WQEFEM50N_digital_email_20180129_EOM598FilingDeadlines_inact")**

Over 30,000 women have reached out to us since Election Day 2016 about running for office. Many of them are ready to run this year.

But if we don't have the resources to help these women meet filing deadlines in time to get their names on the ballot, we won't be able to turn the opportunity of this moment into a true sea change.

The 2018 filing deadlines have already passed for Illinois, Texas, and West Virginia. Let's make sure we can help women in the remaining states get what they need to file their paperwork to run.

**Donate $3 to help reach our $150,000 End-of-Month goal:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Reach-January-Goal](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c541c/24c69fd9/12a1b3a9b/18956af0/652668474/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180128_email_EOMJanuary?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgReachJanuaryGoal&utm_campaign=WQEFEM50N_digital_email_20180129_EOM598FilingDeadlines_inact&source=WQEFEM50N_digital_email_20180129_EOM598FilingDeadlines_inact")**

Thanks -- and go team!

Emily Cain
Executive Director, EMILY's List

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: During Trump's campaign
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Bernie Sanders
body:
Tuesday night is Trump's State of the Union speech. Nobody knows exactly what he will be discussing, but I'm absolutely certain what he will NOT be talking about.
#
Sisters and Brothers -
Tuesday night is Trump's State of the Union speech. Nobody knows exactly what he will be discussing, but I'm absolutely certain what he will NOT be talking about.
He will surely not be apologizing for the many lies he told American voters: how he promised to defend the interests of working people, but then sold them out to Wall Street and the billionaire class.
During his campaign he promised to provide health care to "everybody," but then supported legislation which would have thrown 32 million Americans off of the healthcare they had. Although we managed to stop his effort to repeal the Affordable Care Act, 3.2 million fewer Americans today have health insurance than when Trump first came into office, and millions more will lose their health insurance as a result of the repeal of the individual mandate.
During his campaign he promised to pass tax reform legislation designed to help the middle class. The legislation that he signed will, at the end of 10 years, provide 83 percent of the benefits to the top 1 percent, drive up the deficit by $1.7 trillion and raise taxes for millions of middle class families.
During his campaign he promised to take on the outrageously high prices of the pharmaceutical industry which, he told us, was "getting away with murder." Then, as president, while drug prices continue to soar, he appointed a drug company executive as Secretary of Health and Human Services who worked to triple insulin prices.
During his campaign he promised to take on the greed of Wall Street, but then proceeded to appoint more Wall Street titans to high positions than any president in history. Now, with Wall Street firmly behind him, he is trying to repeal the modest provisions of the Dodd-Frank legislation which provide some consumer protections against Wall Street thievery.
Trump will also not be talking about the role that he has played in significantly lowering the respect that people all over the planet have for the United States. Once, not so many years ago, we were considered to be the political and moral leader in the world, the country most admired. Now, according to a recent Gallup poll, since Trump has been president median approval of U.S. leadership plummeted to 30 percent, down from 48 percent in 2016.
Trump will not talk about his efforts to undermine democracy in the United States and his support for authoritarianism abroad. He will not mention his encouragement to Republican governors to accelerate efforts for voter suppression, and his admiration for the leaders of countries like Russia, Saudi Arabia and the Philippines.
Trump will not talk about his belief that climate change is a "hoax," and his appointment of agency leaders who are undermining our ability to move toward sustainable energy and protect the environment.
Trump will not talk about how he is the first president in modern history who is intentionally trying to divide this nation up based on race, religion, gender, sexual orientation or nation of origin. He will not be mentioning his overt and covert efforts to win the support of white supremacists.
And on and on it goes. I suspect that there will be a lot more interest in what Trump will NOT be talking about in his speech than what he will discuss..
I don't have to tell you what you already know.
The very bad news is that Donald Trump is president of the United States and he is pushing the most dishonest, reactionary and divisive agenda in modern American history.
There is good news, however. And that is that, in an unprecedented way, we are witnessing a revitalization of American democracy with more and more people standing up and fighting back. We are seeing the growth of grassroots organizations and people from every conceivable background starting to run for office - for school board, city council, state legislature and Congress.
And these candidates, from coast to coast, are standing tall for a progressive agenda: Medicare for All, a $15 an hour minimum wage, free tuition at public colleges and universities, transformation of our energy system away from fossil fuels, a woman's right to choose and pay equity, progressive taxation, fair trade, criminal justice and immigration reform and much more.
Yes. The Koch brothers and their billionaire friends are planning to spend some $400 million in the 2018 midterm elections supporting right-wing Republicans. We will beat them, however, because we are in the midst of creating a political revolution. When ordinary people, by the millions, demand economic, racial, social and environmental justice - we will not be denied.
Keep the faith and please remember, despair is not an option.
In Solidarity,
Bernie Sanders

name: Leah
subject: enough yet?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Leah for Senate
body:
Tammy Baldwin and Chuck Schumer have blocked so many important reforms from happening in the Senate. To make it even worse, they shut down the governm

Friend,

Washington dysfunction has held up the Trump agenda since Inauguration Day last year.

We haven’t built the wall. We haven’t passed Kate’s Law. Obamacare hasn’t even been repealed!

Tammy Baldwin and Chuck Schumer have blocked so many important reforms from happening in the Senate. To make it even worse, they shut down the government because they REFUSE to work for the American people.

I think we’ve all had about enough of that. Wisconsin is ready for leadership that gets things done, instead of talking about getting things done.

That’s what I’m going to change when I get to Washington. I just need your help.

body:

**2018 is off to a fast start.**

Team Pence is all over the Sixth District meeting Hoosiers and touring local businesses. We celebrated President Trump's first year in office, and we proudly supported Hoosiers standing up for conservative values at this year's March for Life.

_As an unwavering pro-life, pro-Second Amendment, conservative Hoosier, I am a staunch supporter of the Trump-Pence agenda and will fight alongside the President to Make America Great Again._

**[Will you donate $6 to help me fight for conservative Hoosier values?](http://links.gregpenceforcongress.com/u/click?_t=c56a42391aaa4fc29673628e10310dda&_m=4973b191a93a498ab010277ccb6a6160&_e=Q-3XL7iCWVT4TSBm3sP8GZLjaRnPYO6tlwfRGLjRaqOU7RsgEUiAtf2BOw2lYNG5L0gAG1xMt1eveBLGCei35ugN0WDOP5XB0WA2KL6H1YpydgfWgS_p1xEYzk7EfJMGq2V_XDgko38LsVLY4P455SGjdbsB08wWOQCEBJTWrUbfvTYRvUpXyEUlUyNmBRQbk6zK_l_tWUuSGgf_fSyFpg3wFnmOFeDsaRdnjEqhKsTglQB2h48MUwK22CSS7cEizEHtx30D2reT6G8MsUDsLbrU7eR-qBxrvPh5m7aapTDkesehifDg4xvTPJy6rRtXAWYp4r6LEKpV-IS3U3IUY74HnBs89z5F1heEHHFQY9KOvSqvfXQZhEpsF-iU0c4ciawAzqboQhhXOYFBbrEhsLuqtKHohpAZG4TrHfv4lXlSHy7sH26jgZO2UmE_c3zu)**

Just like our President, I am listening to the REAL Americans – hardworking Hoosiers of the Sixth District – but the rest of Washington isn’t listening.

**I will always fight for Hoosier values and listen to YOU.**

**[Can you chip in even $2 towards my election so I can represent Sixth District Hoosier values in Congress?](http://links.gregpenceforcongress.com/u/click?_t=c56a42391aaa4fc29673628e10310dda&_m=4973b191a93a498ab010277ccb6a6160&_e=Q-3XL7iCWVT4TSBm3sP8GZLjaRnPYO6tlwfRGLjRaqOU7RsgEUiAtf2BOw2lYNG5L0gAG1xMt1eveBLGCei35lVP1a4JmlcPR7ySjqTwKAuWJXBRfuYPQ2kC3oQK4RbQ1mifSoiwSQ9nZY5T9YpO4OP0k0zQzhZrMvcydvTorwMjckogLSh6CEgTEp5wa9bW_sdSytvtgHHDe-6-kpQUkJnQKI5H-Aq75Bsa14zj9NLYzS0oqOxLiU7KVO6J5BZZwPVO_Cfxrcdmjtj3QHa_bQl-Up5Ot3rxW_9RGM6pjSDIIhU9igGKzLYUoRdS95J5lUOVDNx1D8hwTV5NK1q0EnuJOjVHeoLI1mXqxphJjYqK8bY6Scc5HDD3zmWI05QU2Wrf31DytCf350sfE3TIh3j0xezssAVTgWaYmK1FUXITrHtZFeHrO7lnCMrQ7pq2) **

**Here's my promise if you send me to Congress: I will focus on what's most important to Hoosiers, and I will ALWAYS fight alongside President Trump to promote policies that support our veterans and small businesses.**

But - I need you to show YOUR support. I need conservative Hoosiers like you by my side. **[Donate any amount to put Hoosiers and America FIRST. By donating, you can show Washington, D.C. that Team Pence is strong.](http://links.gregpenceforcongress.com/u/click?_t=c56a42391aaa4fc29673628e10310dda&_m=4973b191a93a498ab010277ccb6a6160&_e=Q-3XL7iCWVT4TSBm3sP8GZLjaRnPYO6tlwfRGLjRaqOU7RsgEUiAtf2BOw2lYNG5CYso6m9w3cMwzbw6cvAYY6LFQVXtaisk0zTVw5OQKenoAHG9obqGJafuOz3EWtzWFCzht5KUCkqMg9A9Gyq6kgXR0CZiKjkrBspjmb9ItjeEdtFPKJdycxZf0FZTrX4fSNT_zJZmRJZxiDQqp346EqIR1WZ62YbeK3WBe67I6qC6JXPSzaZWBvkf_KJDw5TOVBMoDe5mrnwcBxtWcYWOJdrkXnMTnMHITRLEE5CE4YPd_gXF7nvAPur-jqHQTzbEPvf64E-JYyfDS86yfYmCqFbAGJ3e5u7tlNBmk3JgQaXMhbhVw2fcJGN5-1EaAiNceIHGvMg8JJZoPVNOU03ICfK-u774r-40bnhfPRabx5s%3D)**

Thank you,
Greg Pence

body:
Don't give Trump the chance to use the State of the Union to "reset" voters' opinion. Help us run ads highlighting his failed record now >>
---
[](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sotu-response-fund?refcode=DPE-20180129-ActionNeed_DS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
**_ABC News: "Amid turmoil, Trump seeking a reset with State of the Union_
[Pitch in $1 now before Tuesday's speech to fund our ads and all our efforts to call out Trump >>](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sotu-response-fund?refcode=DPE-20180129-ActionNeed_DS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")**
Team:

Trump is preparing for his first State of the Union tomorrow -- and _ABC News_ is already reporting that he's trying to use the opportunity to seem "presidential."

Well, we won't give him that chance. Instead, we're going to rile him up with ads highlighting all his failures -- including Twitter ads surrounding the White House to take the fight directly to Trump.

[**Pitch in $1 to hit our $100,000 State of the Union Response Fund goal before tomorrow 's big speech and fund our ads and all our efforts to get in Trump's head.**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sotu-response-fund?refcode=DPE-20180129-ActionNeed_DS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

Trump's first year in office has been a complete failure for working families. His biggest legislative accomplishment this year was a massive giveaway to corporate America, paid for by the middle class.

Now, he's hoping tomorrow's speech will "reset" voters' opinions. So we're running ads to remind Americans of all his failures and steal his thunder before he has a chance to change the story.

**Pitch in $1 right now to our State of the Union Response Fund ****before tomorrow 's speech**** to fund our ads and all our efforts to highlight Trump's failures:**

[**http://www.dscc.org/launch-sotu-ads**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sotu-response-fund?refcode=DPE-20180129-ActionNeed_DS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

Thank you,

Team DSCC

120 Maryland Ave. NE
Washington, DC 20002** Paid for by the DSCC, dscc.org,

name: Jay Inslee
subject: Here's why the new capital budget is a big deal:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
Last year, Republicans blocked any attempt at a budget. This year, we got it done. | [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79cb6/2f5ea88d/738ea201/2cbc9822/4179049069/VEsH/ "https://facebook.com/jayinslee/posts/10156110047868466") Friend, It's progress like this that makes me proud to be your governor. Together, we passed a capital budget that put thousands of Washingtonians to work, all across the state. If you want more affordable housing, better mental health care, and school construction -- you've got it. **This is the power of the Blue Wall.** Last year, Republicans blocked any attempt to pass a construction budget with much-needed funding for infrastructure across the state. But with a united legislature, we were able to move forward with a capital budget that will benefit the entire state. [**It's a big, important deal. Share the story now with your friends and family: Democrats get the job done.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79cb6/2f5ea88d/738ea201/2cbc9822/4179049069/VEsE/ "https://facebook.com/jayinslee/posts/10156110047868466") At a time when Republicans in Washington D.C. would rather shut down government than protect Dreamers and our kids' healthcare, we're focused on getting things done and putting people to work. While D.C. fails to show any real leadership, our state government is making measurable progress.
* In Yakima, students will get the renovations they deserve to combat overcrowding at East Valley High School.
* In Sequim, biologists can finally upgrade the Dungeness Hatchery to improve fish passage.
* In Ephrata, the community can finally replace an aging water line.

We're investing $4.2 billion in more than 1,400 construction projects. We're ensuring safe drinking water, building affordable housing, and improving dozens of schools. This means sustainable infrastructure, and this means jobs: We're creating more than 19,000 jobs in every corner of the state. Dotting the i's and crossing the t's on a bill like this is hard work -- but it's that work which makes sure we're actually serving the people of our state. [**Share the news on Facebook now to spread the word about the progress we're making, together.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79cb6/2f5ea88d/738ea201/2cbc9822/4179049069/VEsF/ "https://facebook.com/jayinslee/posts/10156110047868466") Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [SHARE »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79cb6/2f5ea88d/738ea201/2cbc9822/4179049069/VEsC/ "https://facebook.com/jayinslee/posts/10156110047868466")
